perturbation.original_text: Principal Investigator and MD Anderson shall notify LBIO within twenty-four (24) hours after learning of any serious AE and any special situation report (both as defined in the applicable Protocol) incurred during or as the result of the Study, and provide a written confirmation report of such individual serious adverse event and special situation report promptly thereafter, as well as a monthly listing of all such serious adverse events and special situation reports, by electronic mail to: lionbiosafety@lionbio.com.
perturbation.changed_text: Lead Researcher and MD Anderson shall notify LBIO within seventy-two (72) hours after learning of any serious AE and any special situation report (both as defined in the applicable Protocol) incurred during or as the result of the Study, and provide a written confirmation report of such individual serious adverse event and special situation report promptly thereafter, as well as a bi-monthly listing of all such serious adverse events and special situation reports, by electronic mail to: lionbiosafety@lionbio.com.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Principal Investigator' to 'Lead Researcher' and 'twenty-four (24) hours' to 'seventy-two (72) hours' and 'monthly listing' to 'bi-monthly listing' introduces a contradiction. While Principal Investigator is defined, Lead Researcher is not. This impacts the clarity of who is responsible for reporting adverse events. The change in reporting timeframe directly contradicts the previously defined protocol, creating in-text contradiction as per Section 2.3(a)(ii) ‘the provisions of this Agreement (including each applicable Study Order and Protocol).’
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) MD Anderson shall, and shall cause each of its employees, agents, contractors, and subcontractors performing Research activities or other obligations under this Agreement, including the Principal Investigator (collectively, "Representatives") to, conduct such activities, and use, store and handle all materials used in the performance of activities under this Agreement and each Study Order, or cause the same to be done, in accordance with (i) all applicable laws, regulations, and guidelines, including, to the extent applicable, the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act ("FFCDA"); the anti-kickback and related provisions of the Social Security Act; the Public Health Services Act; the regulations promulgated by the Food and Drug Administration ("FDA"), including 21 C.F.R. Parts 50, 56, and 58, and, with respect to clinical Studies, the requirements of the Statement of Investigator, FDA Form 1572 (as described in 21 312.53), the terms of which are incorporated by reference into any Study Order pertaining to a clinical Study (and the Principal Investigator for any such clinical Study shall complete, sign, and deliver a Form 1572 to LBIO prior to the commencement of such Study); the United States Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act of 1996, as amended by the HITECH Act, including the Standards for Privacy of Individually Identifiable Health Information; the EU Data Protection Directive; and all other applicable privacy, security and data protection laws (collectively, this sub-clause (i), "Laws"), and, as applicable, the quality standards of "Good Clinical Practice" (which term shall mean generally accepted good clinical practices including those set out in the current version of the Declaration of Helsinki and the International Conference on Harmonization Guidelines for Good Clinical Practice in force from time to time and FDA's most recent guidance and regulations concerning current Good Clinical Practice), (ii) the provisions of this Agreement (including each applicable Study Order and Protocol), and (iii) all written instruction from LBIO, as well as MD Anderson's internal policies and procedures to the extent they do not conflict with the foregoing subsections (i) and (ii).
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the reporting timeframe for adverse events from 24 hours to 72 hours and changes the reporting frequency from monthly to bi-monthly. This directly contradicts the requirement in Section (a)(ii) for MD Anderson and its representatives, including the Principal Investigator, to conduct activities in accordance with the provisions of the Agreement, including the Protocol. Since the altered reporting timeframe and frequency are part of the protocol mentioned in the original and changed text, the change introduced contradicts the stated protocol, which creates in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: MD Anderson shall ensure that all patients from whom Patient Materials (as defined below) were obtained, provided their informed consent and authorization for MD Anderson's and Principal Investigator's transfer of the applicable Patient Materials, data, and information to LBIO as called for in any applicable Study Order, LBIO's use of Patient Materials, data, and information, and LBIO's further transfer of the Patient Materials, data, and information to governmental or regulatory authorities and other third parties, as applicable.
perturbation.changed_text: MD Anderson shall ensure that all patients from whom Subject Samples (as defined below) were obtained, provided their informed consent and authorization for MD Anderson's and Designated Personnel's transfer of the applicable Subject Samples, data, and information to LBIO as called for in any applicable Study Order, LBIO's use of Subject Samples, data, and information, and LBIO's further transfer of the Subject Samples, data, and information to governmental or regulatory authorities and other third parties, as applicable.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Patient Materials' to 'Subject Samples' and 'Principal Investigator's' to 'Designated Personnel's' introduces a contradiction. While Patient Materials and Principal Investigator is defined, Subject Samples and Designated Personnel is not. Now, 'Subject Samples' is not defined anywhere in the document, causing ambiguity. The introduction of 'Designated Personnel' creates uncertainty about who is authorized to handle the transfer of sensitive patient data, creating in-text contradiction as per Section 3.3 (c) The Materials provided by or on behalf of LBIO shall only be used as necessary to conduct the Research, in accordance with the Research Plan, this Agreement, all written instructions from LBIO and all Laws and not for any other uses or activities whatsoever, including in connection with research for any third person or entity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (c) The Materials provided by or on behalf of LBIO shall only be used as necessary to conduct the Research, in accordance with the Research Plan, this Agreement, all written instructions from LBIO and all Laws and not for any other uses or activities whatsoever, including in connection with research for any third person or entity. MD Anderson shall maintain control over Materials received by it from or on behalf of LBIO hereunder and shall not transfer any portion of such Materials to any third party for any purpose other than the purposes of performing its obligations under, and in accordance with, this Agreement, the Research Plan, all written instructions from LBIO and all Laws. MD Anderson shall maintain complete and accurate records relating to the disposition of all Materials provided by or on behalf of LBIO. MD Anderson shall return to LBIO all unused supplies of Materials provided by or on behalf of LBIO in accordance with Section 8.4 or at LBIO's earlier request. MD Anderson shall have no right to provide samples of the Materials provided by or on behalf of LBIO (or products created thereby) to any person or entity.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Patient Materials' with 'Subject Samples' and 'Principal Investigator's' with 'Designated Personnel's'. Because 'Subject Samples' and 'Designated Personnel' are not defined, this creates ambiguity and uncertainty. The original text clearly defined 'Patient Materials' and 'Principal Investigator's', thus allowing MD Anderson to maintain control over those materials as stated in the contradicted text. However, with 'Subject Samples' undefined, it's unclear what materials MD Anderson is now responsible for. With 'Designated Personnel' undefined, it's unclear who can handle the transfer of sensitive patient data, creating in-text contradiction as per Section 3.3 (c) regarding proper usage and transfer of materials, creating uncertainty in which the use of material now falls under.

perturbation.original_text: In furtherance of the foregoing, the Parties shall work together in good faith to, as expeditiously as possible following the Effective Date, put in place a power of attorney granted by the System to LBIO for purposes of enabling LBIO to apply for or to pursue any application for any United States or foreign patent, trademark, copyright or other registration covering Inventions or Works assigned to LBIO hereunder in the event that LBIO is unable to secure MD Anderson's assistance in connection with the same.
perturbation.changed_text: In furtherance of the foregoing, the Parties shall work together in good faith to, as expeditiously as possible following the Effective Date, put in place a power of attorney granted by the Network to LBIO for purposes of enabling LBIO to apply for or to pursue any application for any United States or foreign patent, trademark, copyright or other registration covering Innovations or Creations assigned to LBIO hereunder in the event that LBIO is unable to secure MD Anderson's assistance in connection with the same.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'System' to 'Network', 'Inventions' to 'Innovations' and 'Works' to 'Creations' introduces a contradiction. The 'System' specifically refers to 'The University of Texas System', but 'Network' is undefined, causing confusion about who needs to grant power of attorney, creating an in-text contradiction to the legal obligations, also 'Inventions' and 'Works' are assigned to LBIO. Changing these terms to 'Innovations' and 'Creations,' which are undefined, creates uncertainty about what rights LBIO actually owns. Thus creating an in-text contradiction as per Section 7.2 (a) MD Anderson shall promptly make full written disclosure to LBIO, shall hold in trust for the sole right and benefit of LBIO, and hereby assigns, transfers and conveys to LBIO, or its designee, all of MD Anderson's worldwide right, title and interest in and to any and all Inventions and all Intellectual Property Rights therein and relating thereto[, provided that MD Anderson shall retain the right to use any such Invention for internal research, academic, and patient care purposes].
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) MD Anderson shall promptly make full written disclosure to LBIO, shall hold in trust for the sole right and benefit of LBIO, and hereby assigns, transfers and conveys to LBIO, or its designee, all of MD Anderson's worldwide right, title and interest in and to any and all Inventions and all Intellectual Property Rights therein and relating thereto[, provided that MD Anderson shall retain the right to use any such Invention for internal research, academic, and patient care purposes]. MD Anderson further acknowledges and agrees that all original works of authorship that are made by MD Anderson (solely or jointly with others) in the performance of the Research, excluding any publication made in accordance with Section 6.4 (a "Work") and that are protectable by copyright are "works made for hire," as that term is defined in the United States Copyright Act. However, to the extent that any Work may not, by operation of any Laws, be a work made for hire, MD Anderson hereby assigns, transfers and conveys to LBIO all of MD Anderson's worldwide right, title and interest in and to such Work, including all Intellectual Property Rights therein and relating thereto, subject to MD Anderson's right to use such Work for internal research, academic, and non-commercial patient care purposes prior to publication or public disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The change introduces a contradiction because it alters the defined terms, specifically "System" to "Network", "Inventions" to "Innovations" and "Works" to "Creations". The original "System" refers to 'The University of Texas System,' while 'Network' is undefined, creating uncertainty about who needs to grant the power of attorney. The terms 'Inventions' and 'Works' are assigned to LBIO per the contradicted text, but changing these to 'Innovations' and 'Creations,' which are undefined, creates uncertainty about the rights LBIO actually owns. This contradicts section (a) as it creates ambiguity over which property is assigned.
